exam_name: SSC CGL (Combined Graduate Level) Master Framework
exam_description: The SSC CGL represents the pinnacle of administrative recruitment for Group B and C posts in India. This framework provides a deep-dive into the two-tier CBT architecture, the high-stakes Tier 2 merit system, and the qualifying technical modules required for final appointment.

exam_overview_table.Mandatory_Modules: Computer Knowledge & Data Entry Speed Test

pay_level_hierarchy.posts: Assistant Audit Officer, Assistant Accounts Officer
ASO (CSS/MEA/IB), Income Tax Inspector, Excise Inspector, Preventive Officer
Auditor, Accountant, Tax Assistant, Upper Division Clerk

exam_phases.description: Tier 1 is a speed-accuracy test. Marks are NOT used for final merit but are critical for shortlisting. Historically, the UR cutoff stays between 148-155. It requires a balanced attempt across 4 sections.

The 'One Day, One Exam' merit model. Section I and II marks are added for the merit list. Section III (Computer) and DEST are qualifying. Failure in Section III disqualifies you even if you top Section I & II.

mathematics.sub_topics: Percentage, Profit & Loss, Ratio, Time & Work, SI/CI, Average
Basic Identities, Linear Equations, Quadratic Equations

reasoning.sub_topics: Syllogism, Blood Relations, Coding-Decoding, Seating Arrangement
Statement-Assumptions, Cause & Effect, Course of Action
Mirror Images, Paper Folding, Embedded Figures, Dice

english.sub_topics: One Word, Idioms & Phrases, Synonyms, Antonyms, Spellings
Cloze Test, Para Jumbles, Reading Comprehension

normalization_formula_analysis.description: Corrects the disparity in difficulty levels across different shifts. Marks can swing by 15+ based on shift difficulty.
normalization_formula_analysis.strategy: In easy shifts, aim for 100% accuracy as every single error is magnified by the normalization formula.

mentorship_data.title: The 150+ Cutoff Shield
mentorship_data.content: To clear the Tier 1 UR cutoff, you cannot afford to skip General Awareness. Even with near-perfect scores in Math and English, you need at least 20 marks in GA to be safe from normalization volatility.

mentorship_data.title: Mains High-Stakes Penalty
mentorship_data.content: In Tier 2, the penalty is 1 mark for every 3-mark question. This is a 33% penalty (higher than Tier 1). Blind guessing in the General Awareness module can drop you from 'Inspector' to 'No Post' in 5 minutes.

mentorship_data.title: Computer Section: Post-Selection Barrier
mentorship_data.content: Many candidates with 330+ marks in Tier 2 fail to secure a job because they fail the Computer module. Focus on MS-Excel formulas, Shortcuts, and Networking protocols. It is qualifying but high-level.

mentorship_data.title: English: The Ranking Engine
mentorship_data.content: With 45 questions (135 marks) in Tier 2, English is the most stable scoring section. Mastery of English often compensates for a tough Math paper. Focus on Inference-based Reading Comprehension.

rank_booster_tips: Master 'Vedic Math' techniques for squares and cube roots.
Memorize all Trigonometric identities and Geometry theorems.
Revise 2,000+ One Word Substitutions and Idioms from PYQs.
Practice typing 30 WPM daily for the DEST qualifying round.
Use elimination methods for GA Current Affairs questions.
Analyze Tier 2 mocks for time management between Math and Reasoning.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Are Tier 1 marks included in the final merit?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No. Tier 1 is qualifying. Your final rank is determined solely by your Tier 2 Paper-1 score.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is the typing test mandatory for all posts?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes. DEST (Data Entry Speed Test) is mandatory for every single post in the CGL cycle.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there sectional timing in Tier 2?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes. Section I (Math/Reasoning) and Section II (English/GA) have separate 60-minute time blocks.
